text.title: Risk Management Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules grounding the methodology. Every produced register must satisfy all five.

rule.statement: Every register row MUST have a named human owner and an observable trigger condition (metric, calendar event, or signal). Rows missing either are not managed risks — they are wishes.
rule.rationale: Without an owner, no one monitors the trigger; without a trigger, no one knows when to activate the response. Both gaps guarantee the risk materialises without a prepared response.

rule.statement: Every Probability and Impact score MUST carry a one-line rationale citing the source artefact (charter, HR data, vendor SOW, base-rate archive). Scores without rationale converge to Medium and lose discrimination.
rule.rationale: Subjective scoring is anchoring-prone; teams default to Medium without evidence forcing functions, which makes the register useless for prioritisation.

rule.statement: Per-risk EMV (Probability × Impact in currency) MUST be summed into a single contingency line attached to the cost baseline. Per-risk reserves are forbidden.
rule.rationale: Finance only funds aggregate contingency; per-risk reserves create budget gaming and prevent honest portfolio reasoning.

rule.statement: The register MUST be reviewed every weekly status cycle. A register not reviewed within 14 days is flagged stale and rejected by the validator.
rule.rationale: Risks materialise, mutate, and trigger secondaries continuously; a static register represents kickoff state, not today, and silently misleads the team.

rule.statement: Closed risks MUST be retained with an outcome label (`materialized`, `passed_without_triggering`, `merged_into_R-NN`) and a close date. Deletion is forbidden.
rule.rationale: Retained closed risks build the base-rate corpus for future calibration; deletion destroys the audit trail and lessons-learned pool.
